claims.claim: Unified experience memory stores simulation and pseudo-real episodes with gap/calibration fields.
claims.safe_wording: The memory library stores simulation and pseudo-real experiences using a unified schema with gap and calibration fields.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim true real-robot validation from this report.

claims.claim: Real-format import and pseudo-real evidence exercise sim-real pair, gap, and sandbox calibration paths.
claims.safe_wording: The implementation supports real-format episode import and uses pseudo-real evidence to exercise sim-real pairing, gap extraction, and sandbox calibration, and shows calibration consumed by sandbox rollout reports.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim real-robot validation or real-robot success-rate improvement unless true real-source execution reports are added.

claims.claim: Shared real-format memory stores RGB-D, lidar, and wrist-force evidence and can derive conservative sensor-gap summaries.
claims.safe_wording: The implementation supports real-format sensor evidence fields for RGB-D, lidar, and wrist-force observations, and can derive conservative sensor-gap summaries from those stored observations.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim real-robot success-rate improvement or validated sensor-derived calibration effects from this row alone.

claims.claim: Candidate plans can be shadow-rolled out in sandbox and scored before selection.
claims.safe_wording: Candidate plans are shadow-executed in MuJoCo and fused with memory scores before selection.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim a full digital twin or real-world execution success.

claims.claim: Stage-aware retrieval renders planner context separating generation, ranking, rewrite, and writeback evidence.
claims.safe_wording: Planner context is assembled from stage-specific memory evidence separating positive examples, risk priors, critic evidence, and writeback histories.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim a learned multi-stage planner.

claims.claim: Visual keyframes are used as an auxiliary retrieval signal during candidate ranking.
claims.safe_wording: Visual keyframes are indexed and used as an auxiliary retrieval signal during candidate ranking.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim broad multimodal semantic reasoning.

claims.claim: Gap-derived calibration changes sandbox initial state and contributes an explicit risk penalty.
claims.safe_wording: Gap-derived calibration changes sandbox initialization and contributes an explicit risk penalty during candidate evaluation.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim dynamics, friction, or full contact-model calibration; this ablation covers object initial-state shift and score penalty.

claims.claim: LLM-generated lessons are audited for grounding, validity, concision, and conflicts before policy use.
claims.safe_wording: Generated lessons are checked for evidence grounding, candidate/skill validity, concision, and internal conflicts before being used for policy adjustment.
claims.avoid_wording: This is a static quality audit for generated lessons, not proof of learned policy rules.

claims.claim: Sandbox critic identifies risky candidates and increases safety evidence under stress variants.
claims.safe_wording: The sandbox critic identifies unsafe candidates and increases the safety margin in stress reports.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim selection changed if memory-only already selected a safe candidate.

claims.claim: Under adversarial ranking stress, sandbox critic redirects selection away from artificially boosted risky candidates.
claims.safe_wording: Under adversarial ranking stress with artificially boosted risky candidates, sandbox critic can redirect selection away from the risky candidate.
claims.avoid_wording: This stress test uses artificial ranking perturbations; do not claim the same selection change occurs in the unperturbed policy.

claims.claim: Closed-loop writeback stores executed experiences and retrieves them in later ranking passes.
claims.safe_wording: Newly executed experiences can be written back and retrieved by later candidate ranking passes.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim statistically proven long-horizon success-rate improvement.

claims.claim: The experience library represents multiple robot memory types rather than only flat episode logs.
claims.safe_wording: The experience library stores multi-type robot memories, with temporal, spatial, episodic, semantic, perceptual, and sim-real gap evidence represented by explicit fields.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim broad RoboMME-scale coverage or real-robot sensor validation from this report; the report measures field-level coverage in the current library.

claims.claim: Text-semantic summaries provide an auxiliary retrieval signal over structured experience fields.
claims.safe_wording: The system constructs explicit text-semantic summaries from scenario, condition, task, anomaly, failure taxonomy, critic, gap, and retrieval-key fields, and uses TF-IDF vectors indexed by FAISS as an auxiliary semantic retrieval signal.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim this is a learned language encoder or neural embedding benchmark; the report measures lightweight TF-IDF + FAISS semantic retrieval only.

claims.claim: Real LLM-generated recovery-plan candidates can be semantically validated, sandboxed in parallel, critic-ranked, and exported as a validated robot plan.
claims.safe_wording: A configured external LLM generated multiple recovery-plan candidates; the system normalized them, validated skill preconditions/effects, ran parallel MuJoCo sandbox rollouts, ranked by critic/sandbox status, and exported a validated_robot_plan for dry-run dispatch.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim real-robot execution success, arbitrary unseen-skill planning, or statistically proven superiority over all single-plan baselines.

claims.claim: Multi-candidate LLM plan search evaluates more recovery alternatives than a single-plan LLM baseline before selecting a validated plan.
claims.safe_wording: The multi-candidate search evaluates more LLM-generated recovery alternatives than a single-plan baseline, runs valid candidates through parallel sandbox rollout, and selects a critic-approved validated plan.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim a success-rate improvement from this small G3 clean ablation alone; both variants succeeded in the current smoke.

claims.claim: Field-atomic execution experiences are converted into parameter priors for later LLM planning.
claims.safe_wording: The system stores successful and failed field atomic experiences and uses them as explicit parameter priors for later planning.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim this proves improved real-robot success without a real-robot ablation.

claims.claim: Cold-start and writeback rounds show whether field-atomic memory is exposed to the planner as explicit context.
claims.safe_wording: Field atomic writeback can be reloaded as explicit planner_input priors in a subsequent planning round.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim parameter improvement from dry-run LLM if the mock plan is deterministic; use real LLM or varied goals for parameter-change evidence.

claims.claim: Field-atomic execution traces expose action-level tracking error, gripper commands, and direct-qpos usage for debugging.
claims.safe_wording: Field-atomic executions expose action-level trace summaries, including final tracking error, gripper command, control mode, and direct-qpos usage for onsite debugging.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim these summaries prove real-robot tracking accuracy; they summarize MuJoCo or stored experience traces only.

claims.claim: Rewrite-loop ablation reports separate supported component evidence from missing true sequential LLM rewrite evidence.
claims.safe_wording: Existing reports support single-plan validation, multi-candidate critic ranking, memory-backed risk evidence, field-atomic parameter-prior exposure, and a dry-run sequential critic-feedback rewrite attempt.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim critic-feedback rewriting improves recovery unless the underlying report has rewrite_rounds > 0 and final_sandbox_status=accept under a real LLM or clearly stated dry-run setting.

claims.claim: Physical sandbox rollouts can sweep control and scene perturbations to expose sensitivity to pose, delay, gain, and gripper effects.
claims.safe_wording: The physical MuJoCo sandbox can sweep pose, delay, gain, and gripper perturbations and report which failures are sensitive to those controls.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim real-driver calibration or real-robot robustness without measured hardware response data.

claims.claim: Core skill defaults are audited to prefer physical actuator execution over direct-qpos shortcuts.
claims.safe_wording: Core base, torso, arm, gripper, and field atomic defaults have been audited to prefer physical control over direct-qpos shortcuts.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim that every explicit debug or test path is physical-only; the audit concerns defaults, not every possible parameter override.

claims.claim: Memory writeback is auditable through explicit write, skip, merge, and reject decisions.
claims.safe_wording: The write policy is auditable: each candidate entry receives an explicit write, skip, merge, or reject decision with a reason.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim the write policy is learned or globally optimal; it is an explicit engineering gate.

claims.claim: Write-policy pressure tests exercise all lifecycle decisions: write, merge, skip, and reject.
claims.safe_wording: A deterministic pressure test constructs representative experiences that trigger write, merge, skip, and reject decisions, showing that writeback is not a plain append-only log.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim the handcrafted pressure cases prove optimal memory lifecycle policy.

claims.claim: Runtime scene observations have a fixed schema for converting onsite RGB-D/LiDAR outputs into sandbox scene construction inputs.
claims.safe_wording: The implementation defines a field runtime observation template for converting onsite perception outputs into sandbox scene inputs.
claims.avoid_wording: Do not claim automatic high-fidelity scene reconstruction until real perception outputs are mapped and validated onsite.
